question: Does an RNA representation reconstruct the tumor's evolutionary (clonal) state? — the hypothesis's literal claim, tumor-intrinsic by construction
method: within-cohort multi-seed 5-fold CV RidgeCV, OOF Spearman, 500x cohort-internal permutation null
caveat_cross_cohort: Hugo (purity-corrected CCF) and Riaz (purity-free VAF proxy) clonality are NOT cross-cohort comparable (see _joins_provenance.json); Gide has no WES. Reported within-cohort only.
verdict: NULL. OOF Spearman equals the permutation null for every target in both cohorts (all perm_p 0.47-0.93, ns). Best-single-dim in-sample correlations sit at the multiple-comparison null (HUGO) or are on n=10 only (RIAZ expr-PC). The RNA representation does not reconstruct the tumor's clonal/evolutionary state at achievable within-cohort n (21-33). This is the FIRST powered test of the hypothesis's literal 'reconstruct the evolutionary state' claim (prior probe was n=10, |rho|<0.19).
power_note: n=21-33 within cohort; a true |rho|~0.5 would be detectable, |rho|~0.3 marginal. A powered cross-cohort test requires recomputing Hugo+Riaz clonality with one pipeline (PyClone-VI + FACETS/ASCAT) so they are comparable, plus WES on Gide (absent).
